method.name: occtl_pi
method.description: Mathematical constant PI (3.14159265358979323846) shared across public C headers.

method.name: occtl_two_pi
method.description: 2 * PI constant, approximately 6.28318

method.name: occtl_pi_over_two
method.description: OCCTL_PI / 2

method.name: occtl_rad_per_deg
method.description: Radians in one degree.

method.name: occtl_angle_5_deg_rad
method.description: 5 degrees in radians.

method.name: occtl_angle_30_deg_rad
method.description: 30 degrees in radians.

method.name: angle_20_deg_rad
method.description: 20 degrees in radians.

method.name: version_major
method.description: Return the major version number from OCCTL_VERSION_MAJOR.

method.name: version_minor
method.description: Return the minor version number from OCCTL_VERSION_MINOR.

method.name: version_patch
method.description: Return the patch version number from OCCTL_VERSION_PATCH.

method.name: occtl_abi_version
method.description: ABI version. Bumped only on hard breakage. Independent of SemVer. Consumers may compare this against occtl_runtime_abi_version at load time and refuse to proceed on mismatch.

method.name: angle_90_deg_rad
method.description: 90 degrees in radians.

method.name: occtl_angle_1_deg_rad
method.description: 1 degree in radians. Equals OCCTL_RAD_PER_DEG (OCCTL_PI / 180.0).

method.name: occtl_ok
method.description: Returns OCCTL_OK (0): Success.

method.name: occtl_error
method.description: Returns OCCTL_ERROR (1): Generic failure; see occtl_error_last.

method.name: occtl_invalid_argument
method.description: Returns OCCTL_INVALID_ARGUMENT (2): A required pointer was NULL or an argument was malformed.

method.name: occtl_invalid_handle
method.description: Returns OCCTL_INVALID_HANDLE (3): A handle was NULL or had been freed.

method.name: occtl_not_found
method.description: Returns OCCTL_NOT_FOUND (4): Requested entity does not exist.

method.name: occtl_out_of_memory
method.description: Returns OCCTL_OUT_OF_MEMORY (5): Allocation failed.

method.name: occtl_out_of_range
method.description: Returns OCCTL_OUT_OF_RANGE (6): A numeric argument was outside the valid range.

method.name: occtl_not_done
method.description: Returns OCCTL_NOT_DONE (7): An OCCT operation reported IsDone()==false.

method.name: occtl_geometry_invalid
method.description: Returns OCCTL_GEOMETRY_INVALID (8): Input geometry was not well-formed.

method.name: occtl_topology_invalid
method.description: Returns OCCTL_TOPOLOGY_INVALID (9): Input topology was not well-formed.

method.name: occtl_io_error
method.description: Returns OCCTL_IO_ERROR (10): Filesystem or I/O failure.

method.name: occtl_format_error
method.description: Returns OCCTL_FORMAT_ERROR (11): File contents were malformed.

method.name: occtl_unsupported
method.description: Returns OCCTL_UNSUPPORTED (12): Operation not supported in this build/configuration.

method.name: occtl_cancelled
method.description: Returns OCCTL_CANCELLED (13): A cooperative cancellation was honoured.

method.name: occtl_buffer_too_small
method.description: Returns OCCTL_BUFFER_TOO_SMALL (14): Two-call pattern: caller buffer was too small.

method.name: occtl_version_mismatch
method.description: Returns OCCTL_VERSION_MISMATCH (15): Options struct_version is not supported.

method.name: occtl_internal
method.description: Returns OCCTL_INTERNAL (16): C++ exception caught at the ABI boundary.

method.name: occtl_wrong_kind
method.description: Returns OCCTL_WRONG_KIND (17): Handle holds a different kind than the extractor expects.

method.name: occtl_status_reserved_future
method.description: Returns OCCTL_STATUS_RESERVED_FUTURE (0x7fffffff): Sentinel forcing int32_t storage.

method.name: status_to_string
method.description: Returns a human-readable name for a status code. Useful for logging. The string is library-owned and valid for the lifetime of the process; do not free it. Returns "OCCTL_UNKNOWN" if status is not a recognised value. Thread-safe.

method.name: occtl_succeeded
method.description: Returns true if the status indicates success (i.e., status equals OCCTL_OK which is 0).

method.name: occtl_kind_invalid
method.description: Invalid / zero sentinel. Value is 0 so calloc/memset-zero IDs are naturally invalid.

method.name: occtl_kind_solid
method.description: Solid node kind. Value is 1.

method.name: occtl_kind_shell
method.description: Shell node kind. Value is 2.

method.name: occtl_kind_face
method.description: Face node kind. Value is 3.

method.name: occtl_kind_wire
method.description: Wire node kind. Value is 4.

method.name: occtl_kind_edge
method.description: Edge node kind. Value is 5.

method.name: occtl_kind_vertex
method.description: Vertex node kind. Value is 6.

method.name: occtl_kind_compound
method.description: Compound node kind. Value is 7.

method.name: occtl_kind_compsolid
method.description: CompSolid node kind. Value is 8.

method.name: occtl_kind_coedge
method.description: CoEdge (half-edge) node kind. Value is 9.

method.name: occtl_kind_product
method.description: Product (assembly root) node kind. Value is 10.

method.name: occtl_kind_occurrence
method.description: Occurrence (assembly instance) node kind. Value is 11.

method.name: occtl_node_kind_reserved_future
method.description: Reserved future sentinel. Value is 0x7fffffff (2147483647). New kinds are appended before this value.

method.name: occtl_rep_kind_invalid
method.description: Invalid / zero sentinel.

method.name: occtl_rep_kind_surface
method.description: 3D surface geometry.

method.name: occtl_rep_kind_curve3d
method.description: 3D curve geometry.

method.name: occtl_rep_kind_curve2d
method.description: 2D parametric curve on a face (PCurve).

method.name: occtl_rep_kind_triangulation
method.description: Surface triangulation.

method.name: occtl_rep_kind_polygon3d
method.description: 3D polyline along an edge.

method.name: occtl_rep_kind_polygon2d
method.description: 2D polyline.

method.name: occtl_rep_kind_polygon_on_tri
method.description: Polyline indexed onto a triangulation.

method.name: occtl_rep_kind_reserved_future
method.description: Reserved future sentinel value.

method.name: occtl_ref_kind_invalid
method.description: Invalid / zero sentinel.

method.name: occtl_ref_kind_shell
method.description: Usage of a Shell definition.

method.name: occtl_ref_kind_face
method.description: Usage of a Face definition.

method.name: occtl_ref_kind_wire
method.description: Usage of a Wire definition.

method.name: occtl_ref_kind_coedge
method.description: Usage of a CoEdge definition.

method.name: occtl_ref_kind_vertex
method.description: Usage of a Vertex definition.

method.name: occtl_ref_kind_solid
method.description: Usage of a Solid definition.

method.name: occtl_ref_kind_child
method.description: Generic mixed-kind child reference.

method.name: occtl_ref_kind_occurrence
method.description: Usage of an Occurrence.

method.name: occtl_ref_kind_reserved_future
method.description: Reserved future sentinel.

method.name: rep_id_invalid
method.description: Invalid sentinel for occtl_rep_id_t, a zero-initialized 64-bit identifier.

method.name: occtl_error_last
method.description: Returns a dictionary with the thread-local last error info: status (int), message (String), source_bits (int64_t), extended (int64_t).

method.name: occtl_error_clear
method.description: Clears the thread-local last error. After calling this, occtl_error_last will return status 0 (OCCTL_OK).

method.name: error_last
method.description: Returns the thread-local last error. Always returns a non-NULL pointer; an all-zero status field means no error pending on this thread. Valid until the next OCCT-Light call on this thread.

method.name: occtl_runtime_init_info_version_1
method.description: Init info versioning constant. New fields are appended under new version constants in future releases.

method.name: runtime_init_info_init
method.description: Returns a Dictionary representing the default occtl_runtime_init_info_t values as defined by the OCCTL_RUNTIME_INIT_INFO_INIT macro ({OCCTL_RUNTIME_INIT_INFO_VERSION_1, NULL}).

method.name: runtime_init
method.description: Initialises the OCCT-Light runtime. Calling this is optional: every entry point lazy-initialises with defaults if needed. Subsequent calls return OCCTL_INVALID_ARGUMENT and have no effect.

method.name: occtl_uid_invalid
method.description: Returns the invalid UID sentinel value (all zeros).

method.name: occtl_runtime_shutdown
method.description: Releases process-wide runtime state allocated by #occtl_runtime_init. After this returns, OCCT-Light entry points may still be called — they will lazy-initialise again with defaults. Call this only at process teardown when you want a clean state. @threadsafe No (no other thread may issue OCCT-Light calls during shutdown).

method.name: occtl_runtime_abi_version
method.description: Returns the runtime ABI version. Compare to OCCTL_ABI_VERSION at load time and refuse to load if they differ — that protects against mixing a newer header with an older binary.

method.name: runtime_occt_version
method.description: Returns the OCCT version OCCT-Light was built against. Diagnostic only; not part of OCCT-Light's ABI promise. Returns a borrowed pointer to a NUL-terminated UTF-8 string of the form "7.x.y". The string is library-owned and valid for the process lifetime. This function is thread-safe.

method.name: occtl_uid_wire_size
method.description: Number of bytes in the wire-format encoding of an #occtl_uid_t. The wire format is a fixed-width opaque blob (16 bytes); do not parse it. The top 8 bytes carry the current 64-bit packed identity in big-endian order; the bottom 8 bytes are reserved (always zero today) so that future widening of the kind tag or counter field can re-key existing stored UIDs without breaking persistence.

method.name: uid_to_bytes
method.description: Encodes a UID into its 16-byte wire format. Round-trips losslessly through occtl_uid_from_bytes for any valid or invalid UID. Byte order is fixed at big-endian and stable across future ABI versions.

method.name: uid_equal
occtl_uid_from_bytes
method.description: Decodes a 16-byte wire-format UID back into an #occtl_uid_t. Rejects payloads whose reserved bytes are non-zero (returns #OCCTL_FORMAT_ERROR) — those signal a future wider encoding that this build does not understand. An all-zero input returns #OCCTL_UID_INVALID with #OCCTL_OK. @param[in] in_bytes Borrows it. Must point to a readable buffer of at least #OCCTL_UID_WIRE_SIZE bytes. @param[out] out_uid Borrows it. Must be non-NULL. @retval OCCTL_OK On success. @retval OCCTL_INVALID_ARGUMENT @p in_bytes or @p out_uid is NULL. @retval OCCTL_FORMAT_ERROR Reserved bytes are non-zero. @threadsafe Yes. @sa occtl_uid_to_bytes
